payload.text:
마켓 카탈로그에서 스크롤 이미지 디폴트인 scroll_contract.png로 로드하지 않습니다.
id기반으로 utill에서 로드하고 없다면 image 값으로 utill 로드해보세요

**Initiating Image Adjustments** I'm starting to think about modifying the image loading process for "scroll" type items in the Market Catalog. Currently, it's hardcoded to use `scroll_contract.png`. My goal is to determine a better way to handle these images, starting with trying to improve the existing logic. I need to make some progress on how the new logic will work. **Refining Image Prioritization** I've been examining the image loading logic in `MarketItemCard.tsx` and `ShoppingCartDrawer.tsx`. The current implementation has a hardcoded scroll image fallback. I now intend to load images based on ID using the utility function. If the ID-based image fails, I'll then attempt loading from the item's `image` property. The instructions I received clarify this, so I will now focus on the steps for the new logic. **Adjusting Image Loading Order** I've been re-evaluating the image loading order. The current logic in `MarketItemCard.tsx` uses `meta.image` first, then a hardcoded scroll image, and finally an ID-based filename. I realized I must prioritize loading from the ID-based URL initially, and only use the `image` value as a fallback. I've noted I cannot "check" if a file exists synchronously and have adjusted the new logic. The hardcoded scroll image is no longer required. **Optimizing Image Fallbacks** I'm now simplifying the image loading strategy. Considering how React handles fallbacks, I'm shifting to a more straightforward approach. The initial plan was to explicitly check for image existence, but now I think I should instead use the `onError` event for an `` tag to simplify the logic. The logic will be `getAssetUrl(`${item.id}.png`)`, or if that fails, try `getAssetUrl(meta.image)`. **Prioritizing Image Sources** I'm now revising my approach to image loading. Initially, I wanted to change to ID-based loading, but I was reminded to use `meta.image` when it exists. I've re-evaluated the `MarketItemCard.tsx` and `ShoppingCartDrawer.tsx` logic. I will now prioritize the ID-based image loading, and use `meta.image` as the fallback if it fails. I am removing the hardcoded scroll logic. **Revising Fallback Strategy** I'm revisiting the existing image loading. My approach now is to utilize the ID-based image loading, and only fallback to using the item's `image` property if the ID-based image fails. The objective is to replace the hardcoded "scroll" image. The `onError` event for an `` tag will be used as a simple fallback mechanism if the ID-based attempt fails. This streamlines the ternary logic and eliminates the hardcoded image. **Implementing Image Fallback Order** I've been re-evaluating the best approach. I'm focusing on handling image fallbacks and prioritizing sources. I'll load the ID-based image first, and fall back to the `meta.image` if it exists. The hardcoded "scroll" logic will be removed, streamlining the process. In `MarketItemCard.tsx`, I'll use `onError` for fallback. **Prioritizing Primary and Fallback** I'm now thinking about how to handle the image prioritization. The latest thought is to use a state-based approach for image fallback, and simplify the logic with an `onError` handler for the `` tag. Alternatively, I can just perform a simpler priority swap in the string based on ID, using the `meta.image` as the fallback. I will now examine `MarketItemCard.tsx` and `ShoppingCartDrawer.tsx`. **Updating Fallback Logic** I'm now implementing the new image loading strategy. I'm focusing on the ID-based approach with `onError` fallbacks. My plan involves modifying `MarketItemCard.tsx` and `ShoppingCartDrawer.tsx` to prioritize ID-based image loading, and then fall back to `meta.image` if necessary. I'll also check `InventoryDisplay.tsx` and `ItemSelectorList.tsx` for similar adjustments. I'm removing the hardcoded "scroll" logic, in all cases. **Extending Scope Consideration** I'm now expanding the scope to include `InventoryDisplay.tsx` and `ItemSelectorList.tsx`, even though the initial request focused on the "Market Catalog". I realized these files also contain the hardcoded "SCROLL" logic. This expansion aims for consistency and a more comprehensive cleanup. My focus will be on the files listed, and I will be looking for a simpler implementation of the image logic. I will now look for common code in `utils.ts`, to see how it can be adjusted. **Refactoring Image Component** I'm now thinking about how to refactor the image loading. I realize I can avoid a separate component by using an inline solution, which is suitable for these minimal changes. My plan now is to use the ID as the primary key for assets and implement a streamlined ternary within the `` tag using the `onError` event for fallback. I'll be implementing this in `MarketItemCard.tsx` and `ShoppingCartDrawer.tsx`. I'll also check `InventoryDisplay.tsx` and `ItemSelectorList.tsx` for consistency. **Expanding File Coverage** My focus has shifted to the `InventoryDisplay.tsx` file, where I discovered the `SCROLL` image fallback. I will now integrate the new loading logic, which involves loading the ID-based image first and falling back to `meta.image` if necessary. I'm aiming to remove the hardcoded scroll image across all relevant files, which are now being updated for consistency. This will eliminate the dependency on `scroll_contract.png`. **Finalizing Image Loading Logic** I've been finalizing the image loading strategy, and have now identified the core logic needed. I'll load images using the ID-based approach, and will handle instance IDs by using the base ID. The implementation in `MarketItemCard.tsx`, `ShoppingCartDrawer.tsx`, `InventoryDisplay.tsx`, and `ItemSelectorList.tsx` will now prioritize loading an image based on the item's base ID. I plan to use `onError` to fall back to `meta.image` if necessary, so no hardcoded images are needed anymore. **Implementing Primary IDs** I'm now examining how the ID field is used in `MarketItemCard.tsx` and `ShoppingCartDrawer.tsx`. I've confirmed that `item.id` is the appropriate key in `MarketItemCard.tsx`, and that `id` correctly refers to the material ID in `ShoppingCartDrawer.tsx`. I can proceed with the new loading logic. The goal is to fully transition to ID-based loading.

아이템의 아이콘 이미지를 로드할 때, 특정 타입을 기반으로 한 하드코딩된 이미지(`scroll_contract.png`) 대신 **ID 기반의 파일명을 우선적으로 시도**하고, 실패할 경우 메타데이터에 정의된 `image` 경로를 사용하는 방식으로 로직을 개선하겠습니다. ### 제안된 변경 사항 1. **이미지 로드 우선순위 변경**: * **1순위**: `getAssetUrl("${id}.png")` (아이템 ID와 일치하는 고유 이미지) * **2순위 (Fallback)**: `meta.image` 또는 `item.image`가 존재할 경우 해당 이미지 로드 * **기존 하드코딩 제거**: `meta.type === 'SCROLL'`일 때 무조건 `scroll_contract.png`를 보여주던 로직을 삭제합니다. 2. **구현 방식**: * `` 태그의 `src`에 ID 기반 경로를 먼저 설정합니다. * `onError` 핸들러를 사용하여 이미지 로드에 실패했을 때만 메타데이터의 `image` 값으로 소스를 교체합니다. * 이를 통해 각 스크롤 아이템(`scroll_t2`, `scroll_t3` 등)이 고유한 파일명을 가질 경우 이를 먼저 보여주게 됩니다. 3. **대상 파일**: * `components/tabs/market/ui/MarketItemCard.tsx` (마켓 카탈로그 카드) * `components/tabs/market/ui/ShoppingCartDrawer.tsx` (장바구니 항목) * `components/InventoryDisplay.tsx` (창고 화면 - 일관성 유지) * `components/ItemSelectorList.tsx` (아이템 선택 팝업 - 일관성 유지) **DO YOU APPROVE?**

**Analyzing Image Prioritization** I'm currently focused on the item icon loading logic and how to improve it. My primary goal is to optimize image loading, specifically by prioritizing ID-based images like `[id].png`. If that fails, I plan to fall back to a defined process. **Optimizing Image Loading Strategy** I've just refined the image loading strategy. I'm prioritizing ID-based images and, if that fails, will now use the `image` property from item metadata. I'm implementing an `onError` handler on the `` tag to achieve this fallback mechanism. Further, I'm removing the hardcoded `scroll_contract.png` default for `SCROLL` items as a part of this. I will make all changes described to the files indicated. **Simplifying Image Fallbacks** I've decided to simplify the image fallback mechanism. The primary source will always be `${item.id}.png`, and the `onError` event handler will manage the state change to the fallback `meta.image` or the appropriate equipment image if there is no image or recipeId. I'll need to modify both `ShoppingCartDrawer.tsx` and `InventoryDisplay.tsx` to handle these state changes. This should also work with the `MarketItemCard` component. **Streamlining Asset Retrieval** I'm now refining the item image retrieval logic to be cleaner and more reusable. I am considering implementing a helper function or hook for this within `ItemSelectorList.tsx`, aiming for a simplified `onError` based fallback. The current logic uses several different approaches, and I'll streamline that. I intend to use image or recipeId, and the `${item.id.split('_')[0]}.png` as appropriate. **Adjusting Equipment Prioritization** I'm now focusing on the special case of Equipment. I realize that for Equipment images, I should prioritize `recipeId.png` over the instance ID (`id.png`). The current ID-based approach won't work, since those instance IDs are unique. I am aiming to apply this strategy through a helper function and `onError` handler, using `recipeId.png` as the primary image source and falling back to `meta.image` when needed. This approach simplifies the core logic and promotes reusability. **Prioritizing Image Sources** I'm now focusing on integrating the updated `getItemImageUrl` function into `InventoryDisplay.tsx` and `ItemSelectorList.tsx`. I will prioritize images in those components and am preparing to implement an `onError` handler for fallback. I've refactored the function to prioritize `${baseId}.png` as the primary image source, and am working to update `ShoppingCartDrawer.tsx` and `MarketItemCard.tsx` as well. The implementation logic will follow, with changes to `itemImage` initialization and `onError` handlers within these files. **Integrating Scroll Fallback Logic** I'm now integrating the fallback logic for scrolls. I'm taking the insight that the `materials` data often specifies `image: 'scroll_contract.png'` explicitly for scrolls, into account. I'll test the strategy to prioritize `scroll_t2.png` and then the `image` property. If the ID-based image fails, it should then fall back to the desired `scroll_contract.png` default image. I'm getting the base XML ready.

entries.description: 아이템 이미지 로드 로직을 ID 기반 우선 시도 후 메타데이터 image 경로로 폴백하도록 개선하고, SCROLL 타입에 대한 하드코딩을 제거했습니다.
장바구니 내 아이템 이미지 로드 로직을 ID 기반 우선 시도 후 메타데이터 image 경로로 폴백하도록 수정했습니다.
창고 화면의 아이템 이미지 로드 로직을 ID 기반 우선 시도 후 정의된 image로 폴백하도록 업데이트하고, 하드코딩된 경로를 제거했습니다.
아이템 선택 팝업 내 이미지 로드 로직을 ID 기반 우선 시도 후 메타데이터 image로 폴백하도록 업데이트했습니다.

payload.text:
POTIONS에도 tier가 반영되어야합니다 small - tier 1
medium 2, large 3, huge 4

scroll_t2도 tier 1 에 구매 가능
scroll_t3는 tier 2 는 구매가능
이되야합니다

**Analyzing Market Mechanics**

I'm currently focused on the `MarketTab` and specifically the `useMarket.ts` hook. My goal is to integrate a tiering system for potions within the 'SUPPLY' type. Additionally, I'm examining how to adjust the unlock tiers for upgrade scrolls to maintain balance. This will involve careful consideration of existing market mechanics.


**Refining Tier Implementations**

I've been drilling into the tiering discrepancies. It seems the logic for filtering items in `categorizedMarketItems` needs an overhaul. I'm focusing on incorporating tier-based logic for potions (SUPPLY) akin to the existing resource handling. Also, the unlock conditions for upgrade scrolls demand adjustment, specifically for `scroll_t2` and `scroll_t3`. The data in `market-catalog.ts` confirms the existence of tier data for potions, so I can use `meta.tier` across the board, including potions and scrolls.


**Adjusting Scroll Visibility**

I'm now revising scroll visibility based on the existing tier data. The `market-catalog.ts` file shows the intended structure, and the logic in `useMarket.ts` needs a refinement. The issue is that `scroll_t2` should become visible at Tier 1, and `scroll_t3` should show at Tier 2. The existing code adds scrolls without any tier checks.


**Implementing Tier-Based Logic**

I'm now integrating the updated tier logic into `useMarket.ts`. The focus is on refining the `categorizedMarketItems` memo to incorporate tier checks for both resources, potions, and scrolls. I'm implementing a new check so that the code correctly filters items, especially regarding scroll visibility, based on the `meta.tier` property. Now, `scroll_t2` will appear at Tier 1, and `scroll_t3` at Tier 2. The logic includes updated conditional return checks for Techniques.


**Implementing Visibility Filters**

I've streamlined the filtering logic, incorporating clearer tier checks within `useMarket.ts`. I've made sure to factor in the ownership checks for facilities, as well. Now, the code correctly manages item visibility based on the current tier. I'm satisfied that these updates are accurate and maintain balanced market access.


**Extending Visibility Checks**

I'm now extending the visibility filters. The priority is updating the grouping logic in `useMarket.ts` to include tier-based filtering for potions. I've confirmed that the resource grouping already uses `meta.tier`, and now it's about applying this consistently to potions within the `SUPPLY` type. This will involve incorporating the same `currentTier` checks that are applied to resources. I'll need to modify the scroll visibility to reflect their tier-based unlock.


**Validating Group Logic**

I'm verifying that the tiering logic functions as anticipated within the market grouping. I've confirmed that the 'Potions & Supplies' group (`groups.sup`) will dynamically filter its contents based on the current tier. I'm satisfied that this satisfies the requirements. Additionally, I've confirmed the tier level progression, starting at 0 and advancing to Tier 1 upon buying the 'furnace,' ensuring correct unlocks after the tutorial.

아이템 카탈로그의 티어 시스템을 사용자의 요청에 맞춰 강화하겠습니다. 이제 포션(SUPPLY)도 플레이어의 대장간 티어에 따라 순차적으로 해금되며, 업그레이드 스크롤의 구매 가능 시점을 조정하여 성장의 흐름을 더욱 매끄럽게 개선합니다.

### 제안된 변경 사항

1.  **포션(SUPPLY) 티어 필터링 적용**:
    *   기존에는 자원(RESOURCE)만 티어 제한을 받았으나, 이제 포션류도 메타데이터의 `tier` 값을 기준으로 현재 플레이어의 티어(`currentTier`) 이하인 경우에만 상점에 노출됩니다.
    *   결과: Tier 1에서는 Small 포션만, Tier 2에서는 Medium까지 노출되는 방식입니다.

2.  **업그레이드 스크롤 해금 시점 조정**:
    *   **Scroll T2 (Tier 2 해금용)**: 플레이어가 Tier 1에 도달했을 때(용광로 설치 후) 카탈로그에 나타납니다.
    *   **Scroll T3 (Tier 3 해금용)**: 플레이어가 Tier 2에 도달했을 때 카탈로그에 나타납니다.

3.  **필터링 로직 최적화**:
    *   `useMarket.ts` 내의 `categorizedMarketItems` 계산 로직을 수정하여 타입별 티어 체크를 통합 관리합니다.

**DO YOU APPROVE?**

**Revising Market Categorization**

I'm currently working on updating the `categorizedMarketItems` logic within `useMarket.ts`. I'm also considering adjustments to `materials.ts` to integrate tier requirements for potions, aligning them with the supply. Specifically, I plan to map potion sizes (Small, Medium, Large, Huge) to corresponding tiers (1, 2, 3, 4).


**Refining Tier Visibility Logic**

I've just been thinking about how to refine the visibility of scrolls, specifically `scroll_t2` and `scroll_t3`. My current focus is implementing specific tier requirements within `categorizedMarketItems` for `SUPPLY` and `TECHNIQUE` items. Potions have defined tiers in `materials.ts`, and I want to ensure these are correctly checked. The logic will need adjustments to incorporate both resource tier visibility and scroll unlocking based on the current tier.


**Defining Tier Requirements Further**

I'm now focused on the specific implementation details, after reviewing `useMarket.ts`. I need to add a `tier` property to `scroll_t2` (tier 1) and `scroll_t3` (tier 2) in `materials.ts`. I'll update `categorizedMarketItems` to check the `tier` property, only showing items if they meet the current tier, with the logic: `if (meta.tier !== undefined && meta.tier > currentTier) return;` for `RESOURCE`, `SUPPLY`, and `TECHNIQUE` items. This ensures proper item visibility based on the player's progression.


**Finalizing Tiered Scroll Access**

I'm now fully immersed in the scroll unlocking logic. I've updated `materials.ts` to define the correct tier requirements for scrolls, assigning `tier: 1` to `scroll_t2` and `tier: 2` to `scroll_t3`.  My focus has then shifted to integrating these tier checks into the `categorizedMarketItems` function in `useMarket.ts`, ensuring that `SUPPLY` and `TECHNIQUE` items are correctly filtered. I think it is all set!


**Checking Item Visibility Logic**

I'm now fully immersed in the item visibility logic. I've updated `materials.ts` to include the `tier` property for scrolls, with `scroll_t3` set to tier 2 and the other scrolls implicitly tier 1. I've also implemented the tier checks in `categorizedMarketItems` in `useMarket.ts`, which already handles affinity requirements for `TECHNIQUE` items. I'm making sure that even if the tier is 0, the `SUPPLY` section isn't empty, and instead, hides items if they have tier requirements above level 0. I think this all looks good!


**Verifying Visibility Implementation**

I'm now fully immersed in the item visibility logic and have just found a previously untiered item. I'm verifying the current implementation, and making the following updates. Specifically, I'm integrating tier filtering logic into `SUPPLY` and `TECHNIQUE` item types within `useMarket.ts`. Also, I'm checking the `materials.ts` potion tiers. I've confirmed that they're already correctly defined. My plan also involved confirming that items, such as `affinity_debug_gift`, are considered.


**Adjusting Scroll Tier Properties**

I've just added the `tier` properties to the scroll definitions. `scroll_t2` in `materials.ts` now has `tier: 1`, and `scroll_t3` has `tier: 2`. I'm making sure items are visible appropriately based on this. I've also updated the visibility logic, which correctly filters based on the player's current tier.


**Integrating Tier Requirements**

I'm now integrating the tier requirements into the `MARKET_CATALOG` in `data/market/market-catalog.ts`. I need to ensure that the `affinity_debug_gift`, which is classified as `SUPPLY` and has `tier: 1` in `materials.ts`, is correctly displayed when the `currentTier` is at least 1. This means updating `useMarket.ts` to reflect this logic.

entries.description: 업그레이드 스크롤에 티어 정보를 추가하여 상점 해금 시점을 제어할 수 있도록 했습니다. (T2 스크롤은 티어 1에, T3 스크롤은 티어 2에 노출)
상점 아이템 필터링 로직을 수정하여 포션(SUPPLY)과 스크롤(TECHNIQUE)에도 티어 제한을 적용했습니다.
